ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for packaged flavoured milkshake; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve packaged milkshake carton or bottle of approximately 250 ml, equivalent to 250 g, providing around 233 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured milkshake (packaged, any flavour) — HPB Singapore Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for packaged flavoured milkshake (any flavour), CR ID D02020002. All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this entry with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Flavoured milk / chocolate milk analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for generic packaged flavoured milkshake. GI of 41 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for flavoured/chocolate milk (GI ~34–41, mean ~41), which is the closest approved analogous dairy beverage with comparable sugar and fat content. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (41 × 35.98) / 100 ≈ 14.8 for a 250 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (14.07 g/100 g) classifying this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB); sodium is low at 65 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is low (65 mg/100 g), the high sugar load (14.07 g/100 g) classifies this as an SSB, which is independently associated with elevated blood pressure per AHA 2018 guidelines; individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this beverage and choose unsweetened low-fat milk instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (14.07 g/100 g) in liquid form with low dietary fibre (0.7 g/100 g), promoting rapid glucose absorption and significant postprandial glycaemic spikes
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 14.07 g sugar per 100 g delivered as a liquid and only 0.7 g dietary fibre, this packaged milkshake causes rapid postprandial glucose elevation and should be avoided by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; plain unsweetened milk or a high-fibre dairy alternative is strongly preferred.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar content (14.07 g/100 g) contributing to hypertriglyceridaemia and visceral adiposity risk; saturated fat estimated at ~1.6 g/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened dairy beverages like this milkshake is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit intake and substitute with unsweetened, low-fat dairy options.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar content (14.07 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokines including TNF-alpha and IL-6
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The high sugar load in this packaged milkshake can amplify systemic inflammation and worsen RA flares; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage and choose plain yogurt or unsweetened milk for calcium without the inflammatory sugar burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured Milkshake (Packaged, Any Flavour) is a ready-to-drink, commercially produced dairy-based beverage available in a range of flavours including chocolate, strawberry, and vanilla. It is made from whole milk blended with added sugars, flavouring agents, stabilisers, and emulsifiers, then packaged in single-serve cartons or bottles of approximately 250 ml. The texture is smooth and creamy, the taste is sweet and milky, and the product requires no preparation. Per 100 g it provides 93 kcal, 2.96 g protein, 2.56 g fat, 14.39 g carbohydrate, and 14.07 g sugar, with a notable calcium content of 133 mg. The high sugar content (14.07 g/100 g) classifies this product as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under both WHO and AHA definitions. Clinically, the liquid sugar matrix and low dietary fibre (0.7 g/100 g) result in rapid glucose absorption, making this beverage unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. Despite its calcium content, the sugar load outweighs the nutritional benefit for these populations. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 41 (low GI) based on analogous flavoured milk data from the University of Sydney GI Database; however, the high sugar concentration and liquid delivery form mean glycaemic impact per serving remains clinically significant. This product is widely consumed as a snack or meal accompaniment in Singapore and internationally.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in a packaged milkshake?
Is milkshake good for high blood pressure?
Is flavoured milk the same as milkshake?
Can I drink milkshake if I have heart disease?
What is the GI of milkshake?
Is packaged milkshake suitable for children?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D02020002) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous flavoured milk entry as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is documented in notes.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.60 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for flavoured milk (USDA FDC ID 1097512, Chocolate milk, reduced fat) as the HPB Foodo source does not report saturated fat breakdown for this entry; this is an adapted value and may vary by brand and flavour. 2. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this generic packaged milkshake record as of 2026. 3. Iron is reported as 0.0 mg/100 g per the HPB Foodo source; this is a confirmed zero value from the approved source. 4. GI of 41 is an analogous estimate based on flavoured/chocolate milk data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this exact generic packaged milkshake product exists in the approved GI database. GL per 250 g serving = (41 × 35.98) / 100 ≈ 14.8. 5. Halal status is assumed based on the generic dairy beverage category; individual brands should be verified against MUIS certification. 6. The dietary tag 'low_calorie' from the input is considered misleading given 93 kcal/100 g and 233 kcal per 250 g serving; this tag has been removed from the output classification.

Added GI of 41 as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (flavoured/chocolate milk) with full estimation basis documented.
Computed glycemic_load_per_serving of 14.8 for a 250 g serving.

Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with reason and source consulted.
Documented iron_mg = 0.0 as confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source.
